source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A rectangle $A B C D$ with dimensions $16 \mathrm{~cm}$ by $4 \mathrm{~cm}$ was folded along the line MN so that corner C meets corner A. What is the area of the Pentagon ABNMD'?
<image1>

This is a folding/geometry setup with spatial relationships not fully specified in text alone
multi_step: The task combines a fold transformation with an area computation
A solver would need multiple linked geometric steps, such as interpreting the fold condition C meets A, locating the fold line relative to the rectangle, and then computing the pentagon area
The target is not a direct lookup; it requires combining shape structure and quantitative reasoning
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true suggests stable multi-solution potential, though this is only supporting evidence
